name: Visual Orchestration Platforms
description: Low-code and visual workflow builders for AI pipelines — drag-and-drop agent composition, visual debugging, and no-code integration of LLM capabilities. Includes platforms like Make, n8n, Langflow, and Flowise. The pattern matters because it determines who can build and maintain AI workflows — just engineers, or also domain experts and operators.
contrarian_take: Visual orchestration tools are converging on the same node-graph paradigm that failed for ETL (see: Informatica graveyards). The winners will be platforms that stay thin — trigger and glue, not execution — letting real runtimes (Claude Code, dedicated agents) handle the complex logic. The moment a visual tool tries to be the agent runtime, it becomes the bottleneck.
example_signals: Beyond Node Graphs: Comparing Visual AI Orchestration Paradigms for Production Workflows
Langflow vs Code-First Agents: A Performance and Maintainability Comparison
The Thin Orchestration Pattern: Why Trigger-Only Platforms Outperform Full-Stack Visual AI Builders
